results.description: Valid adult lead booker — full info provided
results.answer:
John is 35 years old, which means he is an adult. According to our policy, the lead name on a booking must be an adult (aged 18 or over), so John fully meets this requirement. There are no other eligibility restrictions that would prevent him from making this booking.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: age("c1", 35)
CLAIM: llm_approves_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Minor (age 16) as lead booker — policy violation
results.answer:
Based on our booking policy, Sarah is not eligible to be the lead name on a booking because she is 16 years old. Our policy states that the lead name on a booking **must be an adult** (i.e., 18 or over) at the time of booking. Since Sarah is under 18, she cannot make a booking in her own name.

Additionally, if Sarah were to travel, she would need to be accompanied by an adult, as anyone aged under 18 on a holiday must be accompanied by an adult.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: age("c1", 16)
CLAIM: llm_rejects_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Minor (age 15) with adult companion — allowed
results.answer:
Emma is 15 years old, which means she is under 18 and therefore classified as a minor under the booking policy. The policy states that anyone aged under 18 on a holiday must be accompanied by an adult. Mark is 42 years old and therefore qualifies as an adult companion. Since Emma will be accompanied by Mark, she is permitted to travel.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: age("c1", 15)
CLAIM: has_adult_companion("c1")
CLAIM: llm_approves_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Correct cancellation fee — 65 days before departure (30%)
results.answer:
Based on our cancellation policy, if you cancel **65 days before departure**, this falls within the **69–63 days** bracket, which means a cancellation fee of **30% of the total holiday price** applies.

Please note that to cancel your holiday, you must notify us as soon as possible (or have your travel agency notify us on your behalf), and you will receive a cancellation invoice confirming the termination fee.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b003")
CLAIM: days_until_holiday("b003", 65)
CLAIM: llm_claims_cancellation_fee("c1", "b003", 30)

results.description: Hallucination — wrong fee (50% instead of 30%) at 65 days
Name change fee query
results.answer:
The cost to change a name, passenger, or passenger type on your booking is **£25 per person**, regardless of how far in advance of your departure you make the change. This fee applies whether you are 29 or more days away, 28–15 days away, or 14–0 days away from departure. Please note that if it is simply a spelling correction, no fee is charged.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: llm_claims_fee("c1", "b1", "name_change", 2500)

results.description: Accommodation change within 29 days — treated as cancellation
results.answer:
Accommodation changes requested within 29 days of departure are treated as a cancellation by our policy. Since you are asking about making this change 20 days before departure, which falls within that 29-day window, your request to change accommodation would **not** be processed as an amendment — it would instead be **treated as a cancellation**. This means the standard cancellation termination fees would apply (in your case, 20 days before departure falls in the 28–15 days bracket, meaning a fee of **90%** of your total holiday price).

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: days_until_holiday("b1", 20)
CLAIM: llm_claims_amendment_blocked("c1", "b1", "change_accommodation")

results.description: Deposit only required when >84 days until departure
results.answer:
No, you do not need to pay the full amount now if your holiday is 100 days away. Since your holiday is more than 12 weeks (84 days) away, you are only required to pay a deposit at the time of booking. The full balance must be paid at least 12 weeks (84 days) before your departure. As you still have 100 days until your holiday, you have some time before the full payment is due.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: days_until_holiday("b1", 100)
CLAIM: llm_approves_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Complaint filed 35 days after return — too late
results.answer:
Unfortunately, based on our policy, you would not be able to file a complaint 35 days after returning from your holiday. The policy requires that you contact After-Travel Customer Support **within 28 days of coming home** in order for us to investigate properly. Since 35 days exceeds this 28-day window, your complaint would be outside the permitted timeframe.

If you have an issue, please make sure to raise it as soon as possible — ideally with the supplier and our representative while still on holiday, or through our TUI Experience Centre (open 24/7) if our representative is unavailable.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: complaint_filed_days("c1", "b1", 35)

results.description: Amendment fee — amendment type not specified
results.answer:
Could you please let me know what type of change you'd like to make to your booking? For example:

- **Name/passenger change**
- **Upgrading a service, flight extra, room type, or board basis**
- **Changing the duration, TUI Airways flight time, or travelling on an earlier date**
- **Changing accommodation, departure/destination airport, or a non-TUI flight**
- **Travelling on a later date**

Also, how many days until your departure date? This may affect the fee depending on the type of change.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")

CLAIM: llm_claims_cancellation_fee("c1", "b1", 90)

results.description: ATOL protection for flight-inclusive package
results.answer:
Yes, your holiday is financially protected if TUI becomes insolvent, though the type of protection depends on whether your holiday includes a flight or not.

**If your holiday includes a flight**, it is protected under the ATOL scheme (ATOL number 2524), managed by the Civil Aviation Authority. If TUI or the suppliers on your ATOL Certificate are unable to provide the services due to insolvency, the Trustees of the Air Travel Trust may make a payment to you, or an alternative ATOL holder may step in to provide the services you booked (at no extra cost to you). If neither is possible, you can make a claim under the ATOL scheme (or through your credit card issuer where applicable). You will also receive an ATOL Certificate listing what is financially protected.

**If your holiday does not include a flight**, it is protected by a bond held by ABTA (membership number V5126). In the event of insolvency, ABTA may arrange for your services to continue or provide a suitable alternative at the same cost. If the services cannot be provided, you will be entitled to make a claim under ABTA's Scheme of Protection (or through your payment card issuer where applicable).

In both cases, your money is protected, and there are clear mechanisms in place to either continue your holiday or refund your payments.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: llm_claims_abta_protected("c1", "b1")

results.description: Accommodation change via 'swap hotel' phrasing — LLM rewriter path
results.answer:
Changing your accommodation 14 days before departure falls within the **14-0 days** window in our amendments policy. Unfortunately, a change of accommodation within 28 days of departure is **treated as a cancellation** rather than a standard amendment. This means you would not simply pay an amendment fee — instead, the cancellation terms would apply to your booking, which could result in significant charges up to 100% of your holiday price.

In short, you cannot simply swap hotels at this stage without it being treated as a cancellation of your original booking.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: days_until_holiday("b1", 14)
CLAIM: llm_claims_amendment_blocked("c1", "b1", "change_accommodation")

results.description: Minor travelling alone — 'without any adults' phrasing — LLM rewriter path
results.answer:
Based on TUI's booking policy, a 15-year-old travelling alone is **not permitted**. The policy clearly states that anyone aged under 18 on their holiday must be accompanied by an adult. Since your son is 15 years old, he would need to be accompanied by an adult in order to travel.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: age("c1", 15)
CLAIM: llm_rejects_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Lead name eligibility with 'main person on booking' phrasing — LLM rewriter path
results.answer:
Your daughter, who is 17 years and 3 months old, is not eligible to be the lead name on the booking. The policy is clear that the lead name — the main person on the booking — **must be an adult**. Since your daughter is under 18, she does not meet this requirement and cannot be listed as the lead name. An adult member of your family would need to take on the role of lead name instead.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
CLAIM: age("c1", 17)
CLAIM: llm_rejects_booking("c1", "b1")

results.description: Complex multi-party cancellation with colloquial phrasing
results.answer:
Based on the policy, cancelling **20 days before departure** falls within the **28–15 days** bracket, which means you will be charged a termination fee of **90% of the total holiday price**.

This applies to the whole booking regardless of the ages of the travellers — the fee is calculated as a percentage of the total holiday price, not per person.

CLAIM: booking("c1", "b1")
